revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎮宅霊符神社]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name   = Chintaku Reifu Shrine
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image  = File:Chintakureifu-jinja 201608.jpg
|location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} In'yō-chō 5
|enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} (Chintaku Reifu deity)
|shrine Rank = 
|founded = {{ill|Eikyū|en|Eikyū|qid=Q543201|lt=Eikyū (era)|1=Eikyū}} 5 ({{ill|1117|en|1117|qid=Q19538|lt=1117|1=1117}})
|main hall = 
|annual festival = {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|qid=Q2715|lt=July 31|1=July 31}}
|cultural property = 
|ISO = JP-29
|lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 38.54
|long_deg = 135|long_min = 49|long_sec = 41.73
|location = {{ill|In'yōchō|qid=Q28686104|1=In'yōchō}}
|coordinates = {{Coord|34.67737|N|135.82822|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}
}}
'''Chintaku Reifu Shrine''' (鎮宅霊符神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|In'yō-chō|ja|陰陽町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/In'yō-chō|1=In'yōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28686104}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|p=145|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
This shrine serves as the sacred altar for the protection of {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|qid=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}} practitioners. It was founded in {{ill|Eikyū|en|Eikyū|qid=Q543201|lt=Eikyū (era)|1=Eikyū}} 5 ({{ill|1117|en|1117|qid=Q19538|lt=1117|1=1117}}). The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Gen'yōki|ja|元要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen'yōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "The epidemic deity of Kōfuku-ji, during the reign of Emperor Toba, Eikyū 5, the shrine and altar were established, and the four Onmyōji of Nanto served it"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|p=145|qq=}}. The chief priest concurrently serves as the chief priest of {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|p=145|qq=}}.
The enshrined deity, {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, is the god of the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|qid=Q6158445|lt=Creation of Heaven and Earth (Japanese Mythology)|1=Japanese creation myth}}. In later eras, the deity became syncretized with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}, a deification of the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1211496}}.
The shrine features two pairs of {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=komainu|1=Komainu}}, noted for their smiling expressions{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|p=145|qq=}}{{sfn|Dramatic Nara|p=73|qq=}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
* Main deity - {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} (Chintaku Reifu deity){{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|p=145|qq=}}
== Grounds ==
* Main Hall
* Worship Hall
* {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|qid=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} - Enshrined deities: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Sokotutsu no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu no Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu no Mikoto)
* Shrine Gate - Features a plaque inscribed with "Chintaku Reifu deity"{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|p=145|qq=}}.
== Access ==
* {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|qid=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kōtsū Bus|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} "Minami-Fukuro-chō" bus stop, 3-minute walk ({{OSM route map|start=34.67686,135.82596|goal=34.67737,135.82821|name=Route Directions|qq=}}).
* 11-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|qid=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}} Exit 2 ({{OSM route map|start=34.68414,135.82866|goal=34.67737,135.82821|name=Route Directions|qq=}}).
* 15-minute walk from {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|qid=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}} East Exit ({{OSM route map|start=34.68113,135.81943|goal=34.67737,135.82821|name=Route Directions|qq=}}).
== References ==
{{footnotes help|qq=}}
{{Reflist|qq=}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=1985|page=83|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|qq=}}|qq=}}
* {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Dramatic Nara|editor=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=Keihan Information and Education Publishing|year=2010|page=136|isbn=978-4-87806-601-6|ref={{sfnref|Dramatic Nara|qq=}}|qq=}}
